- `DamageChannelId` (type): type DamageChannelId = string ‚Äî Caller-owned id for a damage channel ‚Äî `"fire"`, `"ballistic"`, `"physical"`, `"corrosive"`. The engine never branches on the value; a channel is whatever a game's data declares.
- `DamageClampConfig` (interface): interface DamageClampConfig ‚Äî Caller data for a flat per-hit damage cap.
- `DamageContext` (interface): interface DamageContext ‚Äî The facts a received-damage modifier predicates over: the incoming `channel`, who dealt it, the statuses the target currently carries, and any caller tags. All caller-owned strings; the engine never assigns meaning to a channel, source, status, or tag.
- `DamageHitInput` (interface): interface DamageHitInput ‚Äî One data-defined damage hit: a channel, a base impact, the target's traits and carried statuses, and the caller-owned matchup/received/status data that shapes the outcome. Everything is serializable; the only non-data input is the injected `rng`, so authority can resolve a hit and replicate the returned instances.
- `DamageHitResolution` (interface): interface DamageHitResolution ‚Äî The full, provenance-rich result of resolving a hit ‚Äî every stage kept for UI and debugging. `impact` is the final number to drain; `matchup`, `received`, and `status` expose exactly how it got there. Authority applies `impact` (plus `received.redirects`/`conversions`) and the returned status `instance`; nothing here mutates game state.
- `DamageInterceptor` (interface): interface DamageInterceptor ‚Äî A named stage in the interception pipeline. The `id` is stable so it can be installed, removed, and cited in provenance.
- `DamageMatchup` (interface): interface DamageMatchup<TChannel extends string = DamageChannelId, TTrait extends string = TargetTraitId> ‚Äî A serializable, data-defined matchup table: `channel ‚Üí trait ‚Üí outputs`. When a hit's channel meets a target carrying several traits, every matching entry's outputs multiply together, so axes compose independently (a trait can halve impact yet double status duration). `default` applies for a channel/trait pair the table does not mention.
- `DamagePipeline` (interface): interface DamagePipeline ‚Äî A mutable ordered interceptor chain that transitions can install into and remove from at runtime.
- `DamagePredicate` (interface): interface DamagePredicate ‚Äî A typed predicate over a {@link DamageContext}. Every listed facet must match (AND); a facet matches when the context value is in the list (channels/sources) or shares any member (targetStatuses/tags). An empty predicate matches every hit.
- `DamageResolution` (interface): interface DamageResolution ‚Äî The outcome of running a pending application through the pipeline: what to apply, what was deferred, and why each value changed.
- `DeathReason` (type): type DeathReason = | { kind: "player_kill"; killerUserId: string; via?: { item?: string } } | { kind: "environment"; source: string } | { kind: "self"; source: string } ‚Äî Why an entity died ‚Äî who or what gets credit, for drop/command rules and the `entity.died` event.
- `EncounterConfig` (interface): interface EncounterConfig<TData = unknown> ‚Äî Non-serializable configuration for an encounter: the authored phase tree plus predicate and spawn-provider registries. Re-supplied alongside a loaded {@link EncounterState}, the way `advanceSpawnDirector` pairs config with state, so the state itself stays pure data.
- `EncounterContext` (interface): interface EncounterContext ‚Äî Context passed to {@link startEncounter} / {@link updateEncounter}: the caller signals predicates evaluate against, plus named `spawnPoints` catalogs (authored in the scene, referenced by key) that spawn providers place against. Never holds coordinates the sequence itself owns.
- `EncounterEvent` (type): type EncounterEvent = | { type: "enter"; phaseId: string; iteration: number } | { type: "spawn"; phaseId: string; provider: string; requests: readonly EncounterSpawnRequest[] } | { type: "complete"; phaseId: string; iteration: number; reason: string } | { type: "done" } ‚Äî A lifecycle event emitted while stepping an encounter, carrying provenance.
- `EncounterPhase` (interface): interface EncounterPhase<TData = unknown> ‚Äî One node in an encounter sequence tree. `data` is caller-owned and opaque to the core. A node with `children` is a group whose children run in order; a node without is a leaf. `completion` gates when the node finishes, `spawn` names an on-enter provider, and `repeat` re-runs the node's subtree that many extra times before advancing.
- `EncounterSignals` (interface): interface EncounterSignals ‚Äî Caller-supplied signals a completion predicate reads. Every field is optional and opaque to the core: `counts` of tagged live entities, continuous `metrics`, cumulative `events`, and boolean `flags`. Deterministic given the same values.
- `EncounterSpawnRequest` (interface): interface EncounterSpawnRequest ‚Äî A single spawn request a provider emits; `ref` is caller-defined, `point` optional.
- `EncounterState` (interface): interface EncounterState<TData = unknown> ‚Äî Full serializable encounter state: the working phase tree (including any injected phases), a per-node runtime map, the active path from root to the deepest open node, accumulated time, and whether the sequence has finished. Round-trips through JSON with no behavioral change; the registries live in {@link EncounterConfig}, not here.

- `EntityRenderCues` (interface): interface EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Per-entity render cues: the motion/animation signal a custom `renderEntity` or first-person viewmodel component needs to drive gait, muzzle flash, reload poses, and hit reactions ‚Äî without diffing the parent group's position itself or reading a game-side module map for attack timing.

- `InterceptDecision` (type): type InterceptDecision = | { kind: "pass" } | { kind: "transform"; amount: number; note?: string } | { kind: "clamp"; max: number; note?: string } | { kind: "redirect"; target: string; note?: string } | { kind: "split"; parts: readonly PendingDamage[]; note?: string } | { kind: "defer"; note?: strin‚Ä¶ ‚Äî What an interceptor decides to do with the pending application it was handed. `split` parts continue at the NEXT interceptor (never re-entering the splitter), which is what keeps the pipeline bounded and recursion-free.
- `InterceptRecord` (interface): interface InterceptRecord ‚Äî One row of provenance: which interceptor acted, what it decided, and the amount before/after it acted.

- `Magazine` (interface): interface Magazine ‚Äî A per-weapon magazine: discrete loaded rounds, a timed reload that refills from a reserve pool, and the reserve-pool interaction itself ‚Äî the primitive that replaces hand-rolling mag size, reload delay, and reserve bookkeeping per game (#536.2).

- `MatchupOutputs` (interface): interface MatchupOutputs ‚Äî The independent, typed outputs a channel-vs-trait matchup can scale. Every field is an optional multiplier that defaults to `1` (identity), so a matchup entry names only the axes it bends. `impact` scales the direct hit, `applyChance` the odds a status lands, `magnitude` and `duration` the applied status itself, and `extra` carries any further caller-defined scalar (knockback, stagger, heat) the engine forwards without interpreting.

- `PhaseStatus` (type): type PhaseStatus = "pending" | "active" | "complete" ‚Äî Lifecycle status of one phase in an encounter sequence: waiting to run, currently running, or finished. Groups and leaves share the same three states.

- `ReceivedDamageResult` (interface): interface ReceivedDamageResult ‚Äî The full, provenance-rich outcome of running received modifiers over one hit.
- `ReceivedModifier` (interface): interface ReceivedModifier ‚Äî One serializable receiver-side rule: an optional predicate, a policy, and an ordering key.
- `ReceivedPolicy` (type): type ReceivedPolicy = | { kind: "scale"; factor: number } | { kind: "flat"; delta: number } | { kind: "cap"; max: number } | { kind: "floor"; min: number } | { kind: "convert"; toChannel: DamageChannelId; portion?: number } | { kind: "redirect"; toTarget: string; portion?: number } | { kind: "immune‚Ä¶ ‚Äî What a matched modifier does to the incoming amount. Amplification is one policy beside reduction ‚Äî both are `scale` (factor `> 1` amplifies, `< 1` reduces). `cap`/`floor` clamp, `flat` adds/subtracts, `convert` moves a portion to another channel on the same target, `redirect` moves a portion to another target, and `immune` zeroes the hit outright.

- `RewardClaimSpec` (interface): interface RewardClaimSpec ‚Äî How a `claimed` allocation exposes its pool: open first-come (`first`) or `reserved` to one recipient.
- `RewardGrant` (interface): interface RewardGrant ‚Äî An authoritative, serializable award of one result stack to one recipient ‚Äî carries `via` (the policy that produced it) and `private` (replication scope) so provenance and visibility survive save/load and viewer projection. Applying a grant twice with the same identity is the caller's idempotency contract; this seam only decides who is owed what.
- `RewardPolicyKind` (type): type RewardPolicyKind = "instanced" | "shared" | "copy" | "roundRobin" | "assigned" | "claimed" ‚Äî The distribution policies allocation ships ‚Äî each a distinct answer to "who gets this result".
- `RewardRecipient` (interface): interface RewardRecipient ‚Äî A participant that may receive rewards ‚Äî an opaque recipient id (player, team, party slot) with an optional split `weight` (shared policy), eligibility `tags`, and an `eligible` gate. Ineligible recipients are excluded before any allocation decision so they never perturb others' streams.
- `RewardResult` (interface): interface RewardResult ‚Äî One indivisible reward to distribute among participants ‚Äî an opaque result id (a rolled drop, a currency bundle, a quest token) with an optional stack `count` and free-form `tags` for eligibility and provenance. Allocation never interprets the id; generation (loot tables, roll formulas) owns what it means, keeping the seam genre-agnostic.
- `RewardShare` (interface): interface RewardShare ‚Äî A `{ result, count }` slice of a reward ‚Äî the unit shared pools and grants are expressed in.
- `ShotOriginPolicy` (type): type ShotOriginPolicy = | { kind: "converge"; muzzle?: EntityPosition; height?: number } | { kind: "eye"; height?: number } | { kind: "legacy" } | { kind: "entity" } | { kind: "entityOffset"; offset: EntityPosition } | { kind: "muzzle"; offset?: EntityPosition } | { kind: "camera"; origin: EntityPos‚Ä¶ ‚Äî How a shot's world-space origin (and optional direction) is resolved before prediction/settlement. - `converge` ‚Äî the shot leaves the gun `muzzle` yet still passes through whatever the shooter's sightline (crosshair) covers: origin is the muzzle offset, direction is bent from the muzzle to the eye ray's aim point. The projectile system's default for a free `{ yaw, pitch }` aim, so a bullet visibly comes from the barrel without missing the reticle. Needs a scene raycast to find the aim point (`convergeShot`); a bare `resolveShot` degrades to a straight muzzle ray. Passes an explicit `{ origin, direction }` aim through untouched. - `eye` ‚Äî `aim.origin` when present, else the shooter's entity position raised to eye height; the shot traces the shooter's sightline, so what the crosshair covers is what gets hit. - `legacy` ‚Äî `aim.origin` when present, else the shooter's raw entity position (feet). - `entity` ‚Äî always the shooter's entity position. - `entityOffset` / `muzzle` ‚Äî entity-local offset rotated by the shooter's yaw (muzzle on a weapon model). - `camera` ‚Äî explicit camera/reticle world origin (and optional direction override). - `world` ‚Äî absolute world origin.
- `SpawnProvider` (type): type SpawnProvider = ( params: Readonly<Record<string, unknown>>, ctx: SpawnProviderContext, ) => readonly EncounterSpawnRequest[] ‚Äî A spawn provider: given the phase's `params` and context, returns the spawn requests to emit on enter. Deterministic; reads authored scene points from `ctx.spawnPoints` rather than embedding coordinates.

- `StatusApplicationSpec` (interface): interface StatusApplicationSpec ‚Äî A serializable, effect-agnostic description of a status a hit tries to apply: an opaque `status` id the game routes to its own effect/DoT pipeline, a base landing `chance`, and the duration/ticks/magnitude/attribution/stacking data the resolver needs. The engine never interprets `status` ‚Äî it is a reference, not a built-in condition.
- `StatusImmunity` (interface): interface StatusImmunity ‚Äî Statuses a target cannot receive at all ‚Äî the immunity half of application policy.
- `StatusInstance` (interface): interface StatusInstance ‚Äî A live status carried by a target ‚Äî the serializable, replicable state a status application mutates.
- `StatusStackPolicy` (type): type StatusStackPolicy = | { kind: "refresh" } | { kind: "stack"; max?: number; add?: number } | { kind: "replace" } | { kind: "ignore" } ‚Äî How a fresh application behaves when the target already carries the status. - `refresh` ‚Äî reset duration, hold the existing stack count. - `stack` ‚Äî add `add` stacks (default 1) up to `max`, and refresh duration. - `replace` ‚Äî overwrite duration and magnitude, stacks reset to 1. - `ignore` ‚Äî leave the existing instance untouched (first application wins).
- `TargetTraitId` (type): type TargetTraitId = string ‚Äî Caller-owned id for a target trait ‚Äî `"armored"`, `"fleshy"`, `"wet"`, `"boss"`. Traits are the receiver-side facets a matchup scores a channel against.

- `TelegraphShape` (type): type TelegraphShape = | { kind: "circle"; radius: number } | { kind: "ring"; radius: number; innerRadius: number } | { kind: "cone"; radius: number; angle: number } | { kind: "line"; length: number; width: number } ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `ThresholdCrossing` (interface): interface ThresholdCrossing ‚Äî One emitted crossing: which threshold, which way, and the value span that produced it.

- `VfxPreset` (interface): interface VfxPreset ‚Äî A named visual flavor for a one-shot `combat.vfx` burst: the archetype (`kind`), a `0xRRGGBB` tint, and optional default `durationMs`/`radius`. Purely presentation ‚Äî a preset never touches damage, range, or any authoritative value; it just answers "what does this attack look like" so a game does not hand-roll colors and archetypes.
- `VfxPresetName` (type): type VfxPresetName = keyof typeof vfxPresets ‚Äî A named key into {@link vfxPresets} (`"arrow" | "fireball" | "lightning" | "web" | "slash" | "shield" | "heal" | ...`).
- `WeaponFireResult` (interface): interface WeaponFireResult<THit> ‚Äî The result of a {@link WeaponRuntime.fire} attempt. On `"fired"`, `hits` is whatever the caller's raycast returned and `resolutions` holds the portable damage provenance for each hit that produced a {@link DamageHitInput}; on any blocked status both are empty and no round was spent.
- `WeaponFireStatus` (type): type WeaponFireStatus = "fired" | "cooling" | "empty" | "reloading" ‚Äî Why a {@link WeaponRuntime.fire} attempt resolved the way it did.
- `WeaponRuntime` (interface): interface WeaponRuntime<TAim, THit> ‚Äî A composed weapon fire controller: a {@link FireCadence} gate, an optional {@link Magazine}, the caller's raycast, and portable {@link resolveDamageHit} damage resolution wired together. The caller retains ownership of models, animations, muzzle flash, audio, input, camera, aiming, and the raycaster itself.
- `WeaponRuntimeConfig` (interface): interface WeaponRuntimeConfig<TAim, THit> ‚Äî Configuration for {@link createWeaponRuntime}. Everything time/ammo-related is plain data; the two callbacks are where the caller keeps ownership of aiming, its own raycaster/projectile query, and how a hit maps to damage. There is deliberately no default weapon, projectile, or damage table here ‚Äî those are game content.
- `activePhaseId` (function): function activePhaseId<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>): string | null ‚Äî The deepest active phase id ‚Äî the completion cursor a HUD reads to label the current wave/phase ‚Äî or null before start and once done.
- `advanceCombo` (function): function advanceCombo(input: AdvanceComboInput): AdvanceComboResult ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `advanceMotionCues` (function): function advanceMotionCues(cues: EntityRenderCues, speed: number, dt: number, tuning?: RenderCueTuning): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Advances `bobPhase` and decays `recoil` from a live `speed` sample (e.g. `groundSpeed(entity)`); leaves event-driven fields untouched.
- `allocateRewards` (function): function allocateRewards(kind: RewardPolicyKind, request: AllocationRequest): AllocationOutcome ‚Äî Allocate reward results among participants under a named policy, returning immediate grants and/or claimable pools. Distribution is deterministic and serializable: the same seeds, recipients, and results yield an identical outcome on host and every peer regardless of join order, so allocation replicates as data without re-rolling. Reward *generation* stays upstream (loot tables, roll formulas); this seam only decides who is owed what.
- `applyRenderAnimationEvent` (function): function applyRenderAnimationEvent(cues: EntityRenderCues, event: string): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Applies a `entity.animation` event (`"fire"` / `"reload"` / `"reloadEnd"`, or any game-defined name) to the cue set. Unknown event names are a no-op.
- `applyRenderDeathEvent` (function): function applyRenderDeathEvent(cues: EntityRenderCues): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Marks the cue set dead after `entity.died`; sticky for the lifetime of the render component.
- `applyRenderHitEvent` (function): function applyRenderHitEvent(cues: EntityRenderCues): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Marks a `combat.hitReaction` pulse; the caller clears `hit` again after its own pulse window.
- `applyStatPoolDelta` (function): function applyStatPoolDelta<TOwnerId extends string, TStatId extends string>(access: StatPoolAccess<TOwnerId, TStatId>, ownerId: TOwnerId, statId: TStatId, amount: number): StatPoolAccessResult ‚Äî Read, transition, and write a caller-owned pool through the structural adapter. Positive amounts increase the resource; negative amounts decrease it. The adapter receives a complete replacement value, never a hidden mutation.

- `convergeShot` (function): function convergeShot(deps: ShotOriginDeps, from: string, aim: Aim, range: number, sightHit: (origin: EntityPosition, direction: EntityPosition) => EntityPosition | null, muzzleOffset?: EntityPosition): ResolvedShot | null ‚Äî Resolves a `converge` shot with scene knowledge: fires from the gun muzzle but bends the direction so the shot passes through the aim point the shooter's eye ray covers. `sightHit` casts the eye ray and returns where it lands (first impact), or `null` to fall back to a point `range` metres down the sightline. A `{ origin, direction }` aim is passed through unchanged (nothing to converge).

- `createAbilityKit` (function): function createAbilityKit(configs: readonly AbilitySlotConfig[], options: AbilityKitOptions = {}): AbilityKit ‚Äî A bar of cooldown-gated abilities the player fires by slot, tracking readiness and cooldown per ability.
- `createAccumulatorMeter` (function): function createAccumulatorMeter(config: AccumulatorMeterConfig): AccumulatorMeter ‚Äî A raw accumulating gauge that crosses named tier thresholds as a value builds, with optional decay ‚Äî the primitive under charge, rage, and combo meters.
- `createAntiOneShotPolicy` (function): function createAntiOneShotPolicy(config: AntiOneShotConfig): AntiOneShotPolicy ‚Äî Compose a reusable anti-one-shot / chip-guard clamp from caller data: while above `guardAboveFraction` a hit cannot drop the target below `leaveFraction`, and after such a save the target gets `recoverMs` of immunity. Below the guard fraction, lethal hits pass unchanged.
- `createBuildupMeter` (function): function createBuildupMeter(config: BuildupMeterConfig): BuildupMeter ‚Äî Accumulate an ailment buildup ‚Äî bleed, freeze, poison ‚Äî that procs a status once it fills, then decays.
- `createCastRunner` (function): function createCastRunner(): CastRunner ‚Äî Run a channeled cast/charge timer that movement or damage can interrupt ‚Äî the spell cast bar.
- `createComboPoints` (function): function createComboPoints(config: ComboPointsConfig): ComboPoints ‚Äî Build and spend finisher points ‚Äî the combo-point economy behind rogue-style builders and spenders.
- `createComboRunner` (function): function createComboRunner(combo: ComboString, anim: AnimationState): ComboRunner ‚Äî Advance a chained melee string from timed inputs, tracking the current step and its cancel/continue windows.
- `createDamageClamp` (function): function createDamageClamp(config: DamageClampConfig): DamageInterceptor ‚Äî A stateless interceptor that caps any single application at `maxPerHit`.
- `createDamagePipeline` (function): function createDamagePipeline(initial?: readonly DamageInterceptor[]): DamagePipeline ‚Äî Create a mutable damage interception chain that state transitions install into and remove from ‚Äî e.g. add an invulnerability interceptor on phase enter and drop it on phase exit. Evaluation order is install order.
- `createDeathSystem` (function): function createDeathSystem(deps: DeathSystemDeps): DeathSystem ‚Äî Resolve entity death and the on-death consequences ‚Äî drops, respawn eligibility, kill credit.
- `createDefensiveWindow` (function): function createDefensiveWindow(config: DefensiveWindowConfig): DefensiveWindow ‚Äî Open a timed defensive window ‚Äî block, parry, or i-frames ‚Äî and test incoming hits against it.
- `createDotField` (function): function createDotField(): DotField ‚Äî Builds an empty {@link DotField}; `apply` DoTs onto it and drain damage each frame with `tick`.
- `createDownedState` (function): function createDownedState(config: DownedConfig): DownedState ‚Äî A downed/bleed-out state that ticks toward death and that teammates can revive before the timer runs out.
- `createEffectSystem` (function): function createEffectSystem(deps: EffectSystemDeps): EffectSystem ‚Äî Resolve direct or area resource effects through caller-owned stat pools, including ordered absorption, restoration, modifiers, and lethal context.
- `createEncounterState` (function): function createEncounterState<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>): EncounterState<TData> ‚Äî Build the initial, serializable state for an encounter from its config. Deep-copies the authored phase tree (so later injection never mutates the config), verifies phase ids are unique, and leaves every node pending. Does not enter the first phase ‚Äî call {@link startEncounter} for that.
- `createEventMeter` (function): function createEventMeter(config: EventMeterConfig): EventMeter ‚Äî A heat/hype gauge that rises as tagged events land and cools between them, firing when it fills or breaks ‚Äî the streak/overdrive meter shooters and fighters hand-roll.
- `createFireCadence` (function): function createFireCadence(config: FireCadenceConfig): FireCadence ‚Äî Build a {@link FireCadence} rate gate. Reach for this instead of hand-tracking a `lastFiredAt` timestamp and comparing against a fire interval per game.
- `createImmunityWindow` (function): function createImmunityWindow(id = "immunity"): ImmunityWindow ‚Äî A per-target immunity/invulnerability window as an ordinary damage policy ‚Äî granted and cleared by state transitions (i-frames, phase invuln), rejecting damage while active. Deterministic (time is passed in) and serializable.
- `createMagazine` (function): function createMagazine(config: MagazineConfig): Magazine ‚Äî Builds a {@link Magazine}: discrete loaded ammo, a timed reload, and reserve-pool interaction.
- `createProjectileSystem` (function): function createProjectileSystem(deps: ProjectileSystemDeps): ProjectileSystem ‚Äî Spawn and advance projectiles each frame, resolving travel, lifetime, and hits.
- `createRegenShield` (function): function createRegenShield(config: RegenShieldConfig): RegenShield ‚Äî Builds a {@link RegenShield} that suppresses regen for `regenDelayMs` after each hit.
- `createResourcePool` (function): function createResourcePool(config: ResourcePoolConfig): ResourcePool ‚Äî A regenerating resource pool ‚Äî mana, stamina, energy ‚Äî that actions spend from and that refills over time.
- `createStaggerMeter` (function): function createStaggerMeter(config: StaggerMeterConfig): StaggerMeter ‚Äî Build a stagger/poise gauge from landed hits toward a break threshold that staggers the target.
- `createStatPool` (function): function createStatPool(input: StatPoolInput): StatPool ‚Äî Normalize caller data into a complete plain-data pool.
- `createStats` (function): function createStats<TStat extends string>(base: Record<TStat, number>, options?: CreateStatsOptions): Stats<TStat> ‚Äî A stat block whose base values take stacking, timed buffs and debuffs, resolving the modified value on read.
- `createThresholdTracker` (function): function createThresholdTracker(config: ThresholdCrossingConfig): ThresholdTracker ‚Äî Track a numeric source against named thresholds and emit ordered crossing events with direction, hysteresis, once/repeat policy, and skipped-threshold handling. Deterministic and serializable; drives phase transitions, armor breaks, resource warnings, and any other threshold consumer.
- `createWeaponRuntime` (function): function createWeaponRuntime<TAim, THit>(config: WeaponRuntimeConfig<TAim, THit>): WeaponRuntime<TAim, THit> ‚Äî Compose a portable weapon fire controller from the existing generic primitives ‚Äî a {@link FireCadence} gate, an optional {@link Magazine}, a caller-provided raycast, and portable {@link resolveDamageHit} damage resolution. No `GameContext`, entity store, renderer, or default weapon is involved; the caller owns aiming, its raycaster, and all presentation, and the result carries full damage provenance for authority to apply and replicate.
- `encounterProgress` (function): function encounterProgress<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>): { completed: number; total: number; done: boolean; } ‚Äî Progress across the whole tree: how many phases have completed out of the total node count, plus whether the encounter has finished. Counts injected phases.
- `eyeHeightFromColliders` (function): function eyeHeightFromColliders(set: EntityColliderSet | null | undefined): number ‚Äî Eye height derived from a collider set: 90% of the tallest hitbox top, or the humanoid default when unknown.
- `filterOutcomeFor` (function): function filterOutcomeFor(outcome: AllocationOutcome, viewerId: string): AllocationOutcome ‚Äî Project an allocation outcome for one viewer, enforcing replication authoritatively: private grants belonging to other recipients are dropped, and pools the viewer cannot claim (not eligible, or reserved for someone else) are hidden ‚Äî so private rewards never leak across the wire while provenance on the viewer's own grants is preserved.
- `forceCompletePhase` (function): function forceCompletePhase<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>, state: EncounterState<TData>, ctx: EncounterContext = {}, phaseId?: string): EncounterStep<TData> ‚Äî Force the deepest active phase (or a named active phase) to complete now, regardless of its predicate ‚Äî the escape hatch for external/scripted completion and failure/retry flows. Advances into the next phase and emits the same lifecycle events as a natural completion. Returns no events if the phase is not currently active. Mutates and returns `state`.
- `impactPresets` (const): const impactPresets: { readonly pickup: { readonly hitstopMs: 0; readonly trauma: 0.15; }; readonly jumpLand: { readonly hitstopMs: 0; readonly trauma: 0.2; }; readonly enemyKilled: { readonly hitstopMs: 40; readonly trauma: 0.3; }; readonly playerHit: { readonly hitstopMs: 70; readonly trauma: 0.4;‚Ä¶ ‚Äî Calibrated per-event impact feel ‚Äî hitstop and trauma numbers harvested from a shipped game-feel reference, not hand-invented per game. `explosion` and `playerHit` are "heavy hit" events (60‚Äì90ms hitstop @ 0.05 timescale); `pickup`/`jumpLand` are light events with no hitstop. Trauma is later clamped to 1.0 by `resolveHitReaction`.
- `injectPhase` (function): function injectPhase<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>, phase: EncounterPhase<TData>, at: PhaseInjectAt): EncounterState<TData> ‚Äî Insert a phase into the working tree at runtime ‚Äî before/after a sibling, or as a child of a group ‚Äî without disturbing already-completed or active nodes. The injected subtree becomes part of the serializable state (so a mid-encounter save preserves it) and is reached in normal order if it sits ahead of the cursor. Ids must stay tree-unique. Mutates and returns `state`.

- `isDodgeable` (function): function isDodgeable(meta: AttackMeta): boolean ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `isParryable` (function): function isParryable(meta: AttackMeta): boolean ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `patchStatPool` (function): function patchStatPool(pool: StatPool, patch: StatPoolPatch): StatPool ‚Äî Apply new bounds/current to a pool, clamping the result and leaving the input untouched.
- `phaseStatus` (function): function phaseStatus<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>, id: string): PhaseStatus | null ‚Äî The runtime status of a phase by id (pending/active/complete), or null if the id is unknown ‚Äî for driving per-phase HUD ticks and objective checklists.
- `resistanceScale` (function): function resistanceScale<TCategory extends string = string, TProperty extends string = string>(matrix: ResistanceMatrix<TCategory, TProperty>, category: TCategory | string, targetProperties: readonly (TProperty | string)[]): number ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `resolveClaim` (function): function resolveClaim(pool: ClaimablePool, claimantId: string, options?: { nowMs?: number; private?: boolean }): ClaimOutcome ‚Äî Resolve an authoritative claim against a pool. Idempotent: re-claiming an already-owned pool returns the same grants without re-awarding, a losing claimant gets no grants, and eligibility, reservation, and expiry are all enforced here ‚Äî the authority, not UI hiding, decides ownership. Returns the updated (serializable) pool plus the grants owed.
- `resolveDamage` (function): function resolveDamage(interceptors: readonly DamageInterceptor[], pending: PendingDamage, ctx: InterceptContext): DamageResolution ‚Äî Run one pending damage application through an ordered interceptor list before lethality resolves, returning the final applications, any deferred ones, and full provenance. Pure and deterministic; bounded by `MAX_INTERCEPT_STEPS`.
- `resolveDamageHit` (function): function resolveDamageHit(input: DamageHitInput): DamageHitResolution ‚Äî Resolve a full damage hit through the data-defined pipeline: score the channel against the target's traits ({@link resolveMatchup}), scale base impact by the matchup's `impact` axis, run receiver-side modifiers ({@link resolveReceivedDamage}), then roll status application scaled by the matchup's `applyChance`/`magnitude`/`duration` axes ({@link resolveStatusApplication}). Pure and deterministic given a seeded `rng`; returns full provenance and never mutates state, so it is safe to run on the authority and replicate the results.
- `resolveDefense` (function): function resolveDefense(input: ResolveDefenseInput): DefenseResolution ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `resolveHitReaction` (function): function resolveHitReaction(config: HitReactionConfig | ImpactPresetName, input: HitReactionInput): HitReaction ‚Äî Resolves hit feel (hitstop, knockback impulse, camera shake) from either a named `impactPresets` event (`resolveHitReaction("explosion", input)`) or a raw `HitReactionConfig` override.
- `resolveMatchup` (function): function resolveMatchup<TChannel extends string = DamageChannelId, TTrait extends string = TargetTraitId>(matchup: DamageMatchup<TChannel, TTrait>, channel: TChannel | string, targetTraits: readonly (TTrait | string)[]): ResolvedMatchup ‚Äî Resolve a channel against a target's traits into independent, multiplied outputs. Pure and order-independent: every trait the channel has an entry for contributes, its axes multiplying into the running product; unmatched traits are skipped, and when nothing matches the table's `default` (or identity) is used. Bounded by the trait count ‚Äî no world scan.
- `resolveReceivedDamage` (function): function resolveReceivedDamage(input: ReceivedDamageInput): ReceivedDamageResult ‚Äî Run receiver-side modifiers over an incoming hit, in ascending `order`, recording every step. Amplification, reduction, caps, conversion, redirection, and immunity are all data ‚Äî the engine applies whichever policies the target's modifiers declare and never branches on channel names. Pure over serializable data with bounded work (one pass over the modifier list); an `immune` policy short-circuits the remaining modifiers.
- `resolveResistance` (function): function resolveResistance<TCategory extends string = string, TProperty extends string = string>(matrix: ResistanceMatrix<TCategory, TProperty>, category: TCategory | string, targetProperties: readonly (TProperty | string)[]): ResistanceResult ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `resolveShot` (function): function resolveShot(deps: ShotOriginDeps, from: string, aim: Aim, policy: ShotOriginPolicy = { kind: "eye" }): ResolvedShot | null ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `resolveStatusApplication` (function): function resolveStatusApplication(input: StatusApplicationInput): StatusApplicationOutcome ‚Äî Resolve one status application: reject on immunity, roll landing chance against injected RNG, scale magnitude/duration by the matchup, then fold into any existing instance per the stacking policy. Deterministic given a seeded `rng`; pure over serializable data, so authority can run it and replicate only the returned `instance`.
- `resolveVfxPreset` (function): function resolveVfxPreset(name: string | undefined, overrides?: Partial<VfxPreset>): VfxPreset ‚Äî Resolve a named visual flavor into a concrete `{ kind, color, durationMs?, radius? }`, with any provided `overrides` winning field by field. An unknown name resolves to {@link DEFAULT_VFX_PRESET} rather than throwing, so a flavor typo degrades to a visible spark. Used by `ctx.scene.entity.vfx({ preset })`; call it directly when you need the raw numbers (e.g. to seed a retained `vfxInstance` from the same vocabulary).
- `rollCheck` (function): function rollCheck(input: CheckInput, rng: () => number = Math.random): CheckResult ‚Äî Resolve a tabletop-style pass/fail roll against a target number with modifiers and crit/fumble bands.
- `startEncounter` (function): function startEncounter<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>, state: EncounterState<TData>, ctx: EncounterContext = {}): EncounterStep<TData> ‚Äî Start a created encounter: enter the first root phase, descending to its first leaf, and return the initial enter/spawn events. A no-op (with no events) on an already-started or empty encounter. Mutates and returns `state`.
- `tierAt` (function): function tierAt(value: number, tiers: readonly MeterTier[]): string | null ‚Äî The highest tier id whose `at` threshold `value` has reached, or `null` below every tier ‚Äî the pure lookup `createAccumulatorMeter`/`createEventMeter` call on every `add`/`tick`.
- `updateEncounter` (function): function updateEncounter<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>, state: EncounterState<TData>, dt: number, ctx: EncounterContext = {}): EncounterStep<TData> ‚Äî Advance an encounter by `dt` seconds against the caller `ctx`. Accumulates time, then repeatedly: evaluates the cursor node's completion predicate; on completion, either re-runs its subtree (`repeat`) or marks it complete and advances to the next sibling ‚Äî bubbling up to parent groups whose children are exhausted ‚Äî entering the next chain and emitting enter/spawn/complete events. Emits a final `done` event when the last phase completes. Bounded per call by node count; mutates and returns `state`.
- `vfxPresets` (const): const vfxPresets: { readonly arrow: { readonly kind: "projectile"; readonly color: 14207392; }; readonly bolt: { readonly kind: "projectile"; readonly color: 16777215; }; readonly fireball: { readonly kind: "projectile"; readonly color: 16738842; readonly durationMs: 460; }; readonly firebolt: { rea‚Ä¶ ‚Äî Ready-made visual flavors for the things players actually shoot, swing, and cast ‚Äî so `ctx.scene.entity.vfx({ preset: "arrow", from, to })` renders a visible bolt with zero tuning, and `"lightning"`, `"web"`, `"slash"`, `"shield"`, `"heal"`, `"explosion"` likewise just work. Each entry only picks a `kind` (`projectile | beam | nova | glow | spark`), a color, and a lifetime; it is visual vocabulary (like named CSS colors), not a gameplay archetype ‚Äî combat numbers stay in the caller's own effect/ability data. Any field passed to `vfx()` overrides the preset, and unknown names fall back to `spark` so a flavor typo still shows *something* rather than nothing.

## @jgengine/core/combat/damageInterceptors

- `AntiOneShotConfig` (interface): interface AntiOneShotConfig ‚Äî Caller data for the anti-one-shot composition: "cannot cross lethal while above X, leave Y, then recover for Z".
- `AntiOneShotPolicy` (interface): interface AntiOneShotPolicy ‚Äî The anti-one-shot policy: an interceptor plus its serializable recovery-immunity state.
- `DamageClampConfig` (interface): interface DamageClampConfig ‚Äî Caller data for a flat per-hit damage cap.
- `DamageInterceptor` (interface): interface DamageInterceptor ‚Äî A named stage in the interception pipeline. The `id` is stable so it can be installed, removed, and cited in provenance.
- `DamagePipeline` (interface): interface DamagePipeline ‚Äî A mutable ordered interceptor chain that transitions can install into and remove from at runtime.
- `DamageResolution` (interface): interface DamageResolution ‚Äî The outcome of running a pending application through the pipeline: what to apply, what was deferred, and why each value changed.
- `ImmunityWindow` (interface): interface ImmunityWindow ‚Äî A per-target timed immunity policy: an interceptor plus grant/query/serialize controls, toggled by transitions.
- `InterceptContext` (interface): interface InterceptContext ‚Äî Read-only context an interceptor may consult ‚Äî deterministic clock and optional health lookup for HP-relative policies.
- `InterceptDecision` (type): type InterceptDecision = | { kind: "pass" } | { kind: "transform"; amount: number; note?: string } | { kind: "clamp"; max: number; note?: string } | { kind: "redirect"; target: string; note?: string } | { kind: "split"; parts: readonly PendingDamage[]; note?: string } | { kind: "defer"; note?: strin‚Ä¶ ‚Äî What an interceptor decides to do with the pending application it was handed. `split` parts continue at the NEXT interceptor (never re-entering the splitter), which is what keeps the pipeline bounded and recursion-free.
- `InterceptRecord` (interface): interface InterceptRecord ‚Äî One row of provenance: which interceptor acted, what it decided, and the amount before/after it acted.
- `MAX_INTERCEPT_STEPS` (const): const MAX_INTERCEPT_STEPS: 256 ‚Äî Upper bound on interceptor evaluations for a single resolution; guards against runaway `split`/`redirect` loops.
- `PendingDamage` (interface): interface PendingDamage ‚Äî Ordered, inspectable damage-resolution interception (#931).
- `createAntiOneShotPolicy` (function): function createAntiOneShotPolicy(config: AntiOneShotConfig): AntiOneShotPolicy ‚Äî Compose a reusable anti-one-shot / chip-guard clamp from caller data: while above `guardAboveFraction` a hit cannot drop the target below `leaveFraction`, and after such a save the target gets `recoverMs` of immunity. Below the guard fraction, lethal hits pass unchanged.
- `createDamageClamp` (function): function createDamageClamp(config: DamageClampConfig): DamageInterceptor ‚Äî A stateless interceptor that caps any single application at `maxPerHit`.
- `createDamagePipeline` (function): function createDamagePipeline(initial?: readonly DamageInterceptor[]): DamagePipeline ‚Äî Create a mutable damage interception chain that state transitions install into and remove from ‚Äî e.g. add an invulnerability interceptor on phase enter and drop it on phase exit. Evaluation order is install order.
- `createImmunityWindow` (function): function createImmunityWindow(id = "immunity"): ImmunityWindow ‚Äî A per-target immunity/invulnerability window as an ordinary damage policy ‚Äî granted and cleared by state transitions (i-frames, phase invuln), rejecting damage while active. Deterministic (time is passed in) and serializable.
- `resolveDamage` (function): function resolveDamage(interceptors: readonly DamageInterceptor[], pending: PendingDamage, ctx: InterceptContext): DamageResolution ‚Äî Run one pending damage application through an ordered interceptor list before lethality resolves, returning the final applications, any deferred ones, and full provenance. Pure and deterministic; bounded by `MAX_INTERCEPT_STEPS`.

## @jgengine/core/combat/damageMatchup

- `DamageChannelId` (type): type DamageChannelId = string ‚Äî Caller-owned id for a damage channel ‚Äî `"fire"`, `"ballistic"`, `"physical"`, `"corrosive"`. The engine never branches on the value; a channel is whatever a game's data declares.
- `DamageMatchup` (interface): interface DamageMatchup<TChannel extends string = DamageChannelId, TTrait extends string = TargetTraitId> ‚Äî A serializable, data-defined matchup table: `channel ‚Üí trait ‚Üí outputs`. When a hit's channel meets a target carrying several traits, every matching entry's outputs multiply together, so axes compose independently (a trait can halve impact yet double status duration). `default` applies for a channel/trait pair the table does not mention.
- `MatchupContribution` (interface): interface MatchupContribution ‚Äî One `(trait ‚Üí outputs)` pair that contributed to a resolved matchup ‚Äî provenance for UI/debug.
- `MatchupOutputs` (interface): interface MatchupOutputs ‚Äî The independent, typed outputs a channel-vs-trait matchup can scale. Every field is an optional multiplier that defaults to `1` (identity), so a matchup entry names only the axes it bends. `impact` scales the direct hit, `applyChance` the odds a status lands, `magnitude` and `duration` the applied status itself, and `extra` carries any further caller-defined scalar (knockback, stagger, heat) the engine forwards without interpreting.
- `ResolvedMatchup` (interface): interface ResolvedMatchup ‚Äî Fully-resolved matchup outputs (every axis populated) plus the contributions that produced them.
- `TargetTraitId` (type): type TargetTraitId = string ‚Äî Caller-owned id for a target trait ‚Äî `"armored"`, `"fleshy"`, `"wet"`, `"boss"`. Traits are the receiver-side facets a matchup scores a channel against.
- `resolveMatchup` (function): function resolveMatchup<TChannel extends string = DamageChannelId, TTrait extends string = TargetTraitId>(matchup: DamageMatchup<TChannel, TTrait>, channel: TChannel | string, targetTraits: readonly (TTrait | string)[]): ResolvedMatchup ‚Äî Resolve a channel against a target's traits into independent, multiplied outputs. Pure and order-independent: every trait the channel has an entry for contributes, its axes multiplying into the running product; unmatched traits are skipped, and when nothing matches the table's `default` (or identity) is used. Bounded by the trait count ‚Äî no world scan.

## @jgengine/core/combat/damageResolution

- `DamageHitInput` (interface): interface DamageHitInput ‚Äî One data-defined damage hit: a channel, a base impact, the target's traits and carried statuses, and the caller-owned matchup/received/status data that shapes the outcome. Everything is serializable; the only non-data input is the injected `rng`, so authority can resolve a hit and replicate the returned instances.
- `DamageHitResolution` (interface): interface DamageHitResolution ‚Äî The full, provenance-rich result of resolving a hit ‚Äî every stage kept for UI and debugging. `impact` is the final number to drain; `matchup`, `received`, and `status` expose exactly how it got there. Authority applies `impact` (plus `received.redirects`/`conversions`) and the returned status `instance`; nothing here mutates game state.
- `resolveDamageHit` (function): function resolveDamageHit(input: DamageHitInput): DamageHitResolution ‚Äî Resolve a full damage hit through the data-defined pipeline: score the channel against the target's traits ({@link resolveMatchup}), scale base impact by the matchup's `impact` axis, run receiver-side modifiers ({@link resolveReceivedDamage}), then roll status application scaled by the matchup's `applyChance`/`magnitude`/`duration` axes ({@link resolveStatusApplication}). Pure and deterministic given a seeded `rng`; returns full provenance and never mutates state, so it is safe to run on the authority and replicate the results.

## @jgengine/core/combat/dotField

## @jgengine/core/combat/encounterSequence

- `BUILTIN_COMPLETION_PREDICATES` (const): const BUILTIN_COMPLETION_PREDICATES: Readonly<Record<string, CompletionPredicate>> ‚Äî Built-in completion predicates covering the common encounter gates: `immediate` (leaf marker), `manual`/`flag` (external event via a caller flag), `timer` (phase seconds), `cleared` (tagged entity count drained), `metric` (threshold on a live value), `event` (cumulative event count), and `quorum` (at least N of nested predicates met). Merge your own over these through {@link EncounterConfig.predicates}.
- `BUILTIN_SPAWN_PROVIDERS` (const): const BUILTIN_SPAWN_PROVIDERS: Readonly<Record<string, SpawnProvider>> ‚Äî Built-in spawn providers. `points` reads a named authored scene point list from context and cycles it to emit `count` requests, keeping coordinates in the scene rather than the sequence. `list` echoes literal requests authored in `params.requests`. Merge your own catalog- or director-backed providers through {@link EncounterConfig.spawnProviders}.
- `CompletionPredicate` (type): type CompletionPredicate = ( params: Readonly<Record<string, unknown>>, ctx: PredicateContext, ) => boolean ‚Äî A completion predicate: pure and deterministic, returns true when the node it gates is finished. Registered under a `kind` and reused across phases with different `params`.
- `EncounterConfig` (interface): interface EncounterConfig<TData = unknown> ‚Äî Non-serializable configuration for an encounter: the authored phase tree plus predicate and spawn-provider registries. Re-supplied alongside a loaded {@link EncounterState}, the way `advanceSpawnDirector` pairs config with state, so the state itself stays pure data.
- `EncounterContext` (interface): interface EncounterContext ‚Äî Context passed to {@link startEncounter} / {@link updateEncounter}: the caller signals predicates evaluate against, plus named `spawnPoints` catalogs (authored in the scene, referenced by key) that spawn providers place against. Never holds coordinates the sequence itself owns.
- `EncounterEvent` (type): type EncounterEvent = | { type: "enter"; phaseId: string; iteration: number } | { type: "spawn"; phaseId: string; provider: string; requests: readonly EncounterSpawnRequest[] } | { type: "complete"; phaseId: string; iteration: number; reason: string } | { type: "done" } ‚Äî A lifecycle event emitted while stepping an encounter, carrying provenance.
- `EncounterPhase` (interface): interface EncounterPhase<TData = unknown> ‚Äî One node in an encounter sequence tree. `data` is caller-owned and opaque to the core. A node with `children` is a group whose children run in order; a node without is a leaf. `completion` gates when the node finishes, `spawn` names an on-enter provider, and `repeat` re-runs the node's subtree that many extra times before advancing.
- `EncounterSignals` (interface): interface EncounterSignals ‚Äî Caller-supplied signals a completion predicate reads. Every field is optional and opaque to the core: `counts` of tagged live entities, continuous `metrics`, cumulative `events`, and boolean `flags`. Deterministic given the same values.
- `EncounterSpawnRequest` (interface): interface EncounterSpawnRequest ‚Äî A single spawn request a provider emits; `ref` is caller-defined, `point` optional.
- `EncounterState` (interface): interface EncounterState<TData = unknown> ‚Äî Full serializable encounter state: the working phase tree (including any injected phases), a per-node runtime map, the active path from root to the deepest open node, accumulated time, and whether the sequence has finished. Round-trips through JSON with no behavioral change; the registries live in {@link EncounterConfig}, not here.
- `EncounterStep` (interface): interface EncounterStep<TData = unknown> ‚Äî Result of a step: the (mutated, returned) state and the events emitted this step.
- `PhaseCompletionRef` (interface): interface PhaseCompletionRef ‚Äî Reference to a registered completion predicate: a `kind` naming the evaluator plus opaque `params` the evaluator reads. The sequence core never interprets `params`; the predicate registered under `kind` does.
- `PhaseInjectAt` (type): type PhaseInjectAt = | { before: string } | { after: string } | { childOf: string; index?: number } ‚Äî Where to insert an injected phase, relative to an existing node by id.
- `PhaseRuntime` (interface): interface PhaseRuntime ‚Äî Per-node runtime record. Serializable ‚Äî holds no functions or tree references.
- `PhaseSpawnRef` (interface): interface PhaseSpawnRef ‚Äî Reference to a registered spawn provider invoked when a phase is entered: a `kind` naming the provider plus opaque `params`. Spawning is one optional node adapter ‚Äî a phase without `spawn` simply emits no spawn requests.
- `PhaseStatus` (type): type PhaseStatus = "pending" | "active" | "complete" ‚Äî Lifecycle status of one phase in an encounter sequence: waiting to run, currently running, or finished. Groups and leaves share the same three states.
- `PredicateContext` (interface): interface PredicateContext ‚Äî Evaluation context a completion predicate receives for the node under test.
- `SpawnProvider` (type): type SpawnProvider = ( params: Readonly<Record<string, unknown>>, ctx: SpawnProviderContext, ) => readonly EncounterSpawnRequest[] ‚Äî A spawn provider: given the phase's `params` and context, returns the spawn requests to emit on enter. Deterministic; reads authored scene points from `ctx.spawnPoints` rather than embedding coordinates.
- `SpawnProviderContext` (interface): interface SpawnProviderContext ‚Äî Evaluation context a spawn provider receives when its phase is entered.
- `activePhaseId` (function): function activePhaseId<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>): string | null ‚Äî The deepest active phase id ‚Äî the completion cursor a HUD reads to label the current wave/phase ‚Äî or null before start and once done.
- `createEncounterState` (function): function createEncounterState<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>): EncounterState<TData> ‚Äî Build the initial, serializable state for an encounter from its config. Deep-copies the authored phase tree (so later injection never mutates the config), verifies phase ids are unique, and leaves every node pending. Does not enter the first phase ‚Äî call {@link startEncounter} for that.
- `encounterProgress` (function): function encounterProgress<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>): { completed: number; total: number; done: boolean; } ‚Äî Progress across the whole tree: how many phases have completed out of the total node count, plus whether the encounter has finished. Counts injected phases.
- `forceCompletePhase` (function): function forceCompletePhase<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>, state: EncounterState<TData>, ctx: EncounterContext = {}, phaseId?: string): EncounterStep<TData> ‚Äî Force the deepest active phase (or a named active phase) to complete now, regardless of its predicate ‚Äî the escape hatch for external/scripted completion and failure/retry flows. Advances into the next phase and emits the same lifecycle events as a natural completion. Returns no events if the phase is not currently active. Mutates and returns `state`.
- `injectPhase` (function): function injectPhase<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>, phase: EncounterPhase<TData>, at: PhaseInjectAt): EncounterState<TData> ‚Äî Insert a phase into the working tree at runtime ‚Äî before/after a sibling, or as a child of a group ‚Äî without disturbing already-completed or active nodes. The injected subtree becomes part of the serializable state (so a mid-encounter save preserves it) and is reached in normal order if it sits ahead of the cursor. Ids must stay tree-unique. Mutates and returns `state`.
- `phaseStatus` (function): function phaseStatus<TData>(state: EncounterState<TData>, id: string): PhaseStatus | null ‚Äî The runtime status of a phase by id (pending/active/complete), or null if the id is unknown ‚Äî for driving per-phase HUD ticks and objective checklists.
- `startEncounter` (function): function startEncounter<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>, state: EncounterState<TData>, ctx: EncounterContext = {}): EncounterStep<TData> ‚Äî Start a created encounter: enter the first root phase, descending to its first leaf, and return the initial enter/spawn events. A no-op (with no events) on an already-started or empty encounter. Mutates and returns `state`.
- `updateEncounter` (function): function updateEncounter<TData>(config: EncounterConfig<TData>, state: EncounterState<TData>, dt: number, ctx: EncounterContext = {}): EncounterStep<TData> ‚Äî Advance an encounter by `dt` seconds against the caller `ctx`. Accumulates time, then repeatedly: evaluates the cursor node's completion predicate; on completion, either re-runs its subtree (`repeat`) or marks it complete and advances to the next sibling ‚Äî bubbling up to parent groups whose children are exhausted ‚Äî entering the next chain and emitting enter/spawn/complete events. Emits a final `done` event when the last phase completes. Bounded per call by node count; mutates and returns `state`.

Trauma is later clamped to 1.0 by `resolveHitReaction`.

## @jgengine/core/combat/receivedDamage

- `DamageContext` (interface): interface DamageContext ‚Äî The facts a received-damage modifier predicates over: the incoming `channel`, who dealt it, the statuses the target currently carries, and any caller tags. All caller-owned strings; the engine never assigns meaning to a channel, source, status, or tag.
- `DamageDivert` (interface): interface DamageDivert ‚Äî A portion of the hit sent elsewhere by a `redirect`/`convert` policy.
- `DamagePredicate` (interface): interface DamagePredicate ‚Äî A typed predicate over a {@link DamageContext}. Every listed facet must match (AND); a facet matches when the context value is in the list (channels/sources) or shares any member (targetStatuses/tags). An empty predicate matches every hit.
- `ReceivedDamageInput` (interface): interface ReceivedDamageInput ‚Äî The incoming amount, its {@link DamageContext}, and the receiver's modifier list to run over it.
- `ReceivedDamageResult` (interface): interface ReceivedDamageResult ‚Äî The full, provenance-rich outcome of running received modifiers over one hit.
- `ReceivedModifier` (interface): interface ReceivedModifier ‚Äî One serializable receiver-side rule: an optional predicate, a policy, and an ordering key.
- `ReceivedPolicy` (type): type ReceivedPolicy = | { kind: "scale"; factor: number } | { kind: "flat"; delta: number } | { kind: "cap"; max: number } | { kind: "floor"; min: number } | { kind: "convert"; toChannel: DamageChannelId; portion?: number } | { kind: "redirect"; toTarget: string; portion?: number } | { kind: "immune‚Ä¶ ‚Äî What a matched modifier does to the incoming amount. Amplification is one policy beside reduction ‚Äî both are `scale` (factor `> 1` amplifies, `< 1` reduces). `cap`/`floor` clamp, `flat` adds/subtracts, `convert` moves a portion to another channel on the same target, `redirect` moves a portion to another target, and `immune` zeroes the hit outright.
- `ReceivedStep` (interface): interface ReceivedStep ‚Äî One recorded transformation ‚Äî the provenance a UI or debugger reads to explain a final number.
- `resolveReceivedDamage` (function): function resolveReceivedDamage(input: ReceivedDamageInput): ReceivedDamageResult ‚Äî Run receiver-side modifiers over an incoming hit, in ascending `order`, recording every step. Amplification, reduction, caps, conversion, redirection, and immunity are all data ‚Äî the engine applies whichever policies the target's modifiers declare and never branches on channel names. Pure over serializable data with bounded work (one pass over the modifier list); an `immune` policy short-circuits the remaining modifiers.

## @jgengine/core/combat/regenShield

- `RegenShield` (interface): interface RegenShield ‚Äî A shield pool that stops regenerating for `regenDelayMs` after every hit, then refills at `regenPerSecond` ‚Äî the delayed-regen primitive that replaces snapshot-comparing stat values per tick to detect "damage taken" (#536.3). `damage` resets the grace timer; `tick` counts it down and regenerates once it elapses.
- `RegenShieldConfig` (interface): interface RegenShieldConfig ‚Äî Tuning for `createRegenShield`: pool size, refill rate, and the post-damage grace period.
- `createRegenShield` (function): function createRegenShield(config: RegenShieldConfig): RegenShield ‚Äî Builds a {@link RegenShield} that suppresses regen for `regenDelayMs` after each hit.

## @jgengine/core/combat/renderCues

- `DEFAULT_CYCLES_PER_UNIT` (const): const DEFAULT_CYCLES_PER_UNIT: 0.6 ‚Äî Default `RenderCueTuning.cyclesPerUnit`.
- `DEFAULT_FIRE_PULSE_SECONDS` (const): const DEFAULT_FIRE_PULSE_SECONDS: 0.12 ‚Äî Default `RenderCueTuning.firePulseSeconds`.
- `DEFAULT_HIT_PULSE_SECONDS` (const): const DEFAULT_HIT_PULSE_SECONDS: 0.2 ‚Äî Default `RenderCueTuning.hitPulseSeconds`.
- `DEFAULT_RECOIL_DECAY_PER_SECOND` (const): const DEFAULT_RECOIL_DECAY_PER_SECOND: 6 ‚Äî Default `RenderCueTuning.recoilDecayPerSecond`.
- `DEFAULT_RENDER_CUES` (const): const DEFAULT_RENDER_CUES: Readonly<EntityRenderCues> ‚Äî Neutral starting cue set: idle, unarmed, undamaged.
- `EntityRenderCues` (interface): interface EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Per-entity render cues: the motion/animation signal a custom `renderEntity` or first-person viewmodel component needs to drive gait, muzzle flash, reload poses, and hit reactions ‚Äî without diffing the parent group's position itself or reading a game-side module map for attack timing.
- `RenderCueTuning` (interface): interface RenderCueTuning ‚Äî Tuning knobs for `advanceMotionCues` / `useEntityRenderCues`; every field has a default, override only what a weapon/rig's feel needs to differ.
- `advanceMotionCues` (function): function advanceMotionCues(cues: EntityRenderCues, speed: number, dt: number, tuning?: RenderCueTuning): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Advances `bobPhase` and decays `recoil` from a live `speed` sample (e.g. `groundSpeed(entity)`); leaves event-driven fields untouched.
- `applyRenderAnimationEvent` (function): function applyRenderAnimationEvent(cues: EntityRenderCues, event: string): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Applies a `entity.animation` event (`"fire"` / `"reload"` / `"reloadEnd"`, or any game-defined name) to the cue set. Unknown event names are a no-op.
- `applyRenderDeathEvent` (function): function applyRenderDeathEvent(cues: EntityRenderCues): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Marks the cue set dead after `entity.died`; sticky for the lifetime of the render component.
- `applyRenderHitEvent` (function): function applyRenderHitEvent(cues: EntityRenderCues): EntityRenderCues ‚Äî Marks a `combat.hitReaction` pulse; the caller clears `hit` again after its own pulse window.

## @jgengine/core/combat/rewardAllocation

- `AllocationOutcome` (interface): interface AllocationOutcome ‚Äî The serializable result of an allocation pass ‚Äî immediate `grants` plus any deferred `pools`.
- `AllocationRequest` (interface): interface AllocationRequest ‚Äî The inputs one allocation pass consumes. `allocationSeed` governs distribution decisions (ordering, round-robin rotation, remainder splits, reservation) while the separate optional `generationSeed` seeds per-recipient `instanced` generation ‚Äî keeping join order from perturbing other players' rolls. Provide `results` for pre-rolled policies, `generate` for `instanced`, `assignment` for `assigned`, and `claim` for `claimed`.
- `ClaimOutcome` (interface): interface ClaimOutcome ‚Äî The outcome of a claim attempt ‚Äî the updated pool and the grants owed (empty when the claim is refused).
- `ClaimablePool` (interface): interface ClaimablePool ‚Äî A serializable pool of results left open for later claiming ‚Äî first-come contested drops or a `reservedFor` personal reward. Tracks `eligible` claimants, optional `expiresAtMs`, and the authoritative `claimedBy` winner so reconnect and duplicate-claim attempts resolve idempotently.
- `RewardClaimSpec` (interface): interface RewardClaimSpec ‚Äî How a `claimed` allocation exposes its pool: open first-come (`first`) or `reserved` to one recipient.
- `RewardGrant` (interface): interface RewardGrant ‚Äî An authoritative, serializable award of one result stack to one recipient ‚Äî carries `via` (the policy that produced it) and `private` (replication scope) so provenance and visibility survive save/load and viewer projection. Applying a grant twice with the same identity is the caller's idempotency contract; this seam only decides who is owed what.
- `RewardPolicyKind` (type): type RewardPolicyKind = "instanced" | "shared" | "copy" | "roundRobin" | "assigned" | "claimed" ‚Äî The distribution policies allocation ships ‚Äî each a distinct answer to "who gets this result".
- `RewardRecipient` (interface): interface RewardRecipient ‚Äî A participant that may receive rewards ‚Äî an opaque recipient id (player, team, party slot) with an optional split `weight` (shared policy), eligibility `tags`, and an `eligible` gate. Ineligible recipients are excluded before any allocation decision so they never perturb others' streams.
- `RewardResult` (interface): interface RewardResult ‚Äî One indivisible reward to distribute among participants ‚Äî an opaque result id (a rolled drop, a currency bundle, a quest token) with an optional stack `count` and free-form `tags` for eligibility and provenance. Allocation never interprets the id; generation (loot tables, roll formulas) owns what it means, keeping the seam genre-agnostic.
- `RewardShare` (interface): interface RewardShare ‚Äî A `{ result, count }` slice of a reward ‚Äî the unit shared pools and grants are expressed in.
- `allocateRewards` (function): function allocateRewards(kind: RewardPolicyKind, request: AllocationRequest): AllocationOutcome ‚Äî Allocate reward results among participants under a named policy, returning immediate grants and/or claimable pools. Distribution is deterministic and serializable: the same seeds, recipients, and results yield an identical outcome on host and every peer regardless of join order, so allocation replicates as data without re-rolling. Reward *generation* stays upstream (loot tables, roll formulas); this seam only decides who is owed what.
- `filterOutcomeFor` (function): function filterOutcomeFor(outcome: AllocationOutcome, viewerId: string): AllocationOutcome ‚Äî Project an allocation outcome for one viewer, enforcing replication authoritatively: private grants belonging to other recipients are dropped, and pools the viewer cannot claim (not eligible, or reserved for someone else) are hidden ‚Äî so private rewards never leak across the wire while provenance on the viewer's own grants is preserved.
- `resolveClaim` (function): function resolveClaim(pool: ClaimablePool, claimantId: string, options?: { nowMs?: number; private?: boolean }): ClaimOutcome ‚Äî Resolve an authoritative claim against a pool. Idempotent: re-claiming an already-owned pool returns the same grants without re-awarding, a losing claimant gets no grants, and eligibility, reservation, and expiry are all enforced here ‚Äî the authority, not UI hiding, decides ownership. Returns the updated (serializable) pool plus the grants owed.

## @jgengine/core/combat/shotOrigin

- `DEFAULT_EYE_HEIGHT` (const): const DEFAULT_EYE_HEIGHT: number ‚Äî Shot-origin and first-person camera eye height above an entity's position: 90% of the default 1.8m hitbox top.
- `ResolvedShot` (interface): interface ResolvedShot ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `ShotOriginDeps` (interface): interface ShotOriginDeps ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `ShotOriginPolicy` (type): type ShotOriginPolicy = | { kind: "converge"; muzzle?: EntityPosition; height?: number } | { kind: "eye"; height?: number } | { kind: "legacy" } | { kind: "entity" } | { kind: "entityOffset"; offset: EntityPosition } | { kind: "muzzle"; offset?: EntityPosition } | { kind: "camera"; origin: EntityPos‚Ä¶ ‚Äî How a shot's world-space origin (and optional direction) is resolved before prediction/settlement. - `converge` ‚Äî the shot leaves the gun `muzzle` yet still passes through whatever the shooter's sightline (crosshair) covers: origin is the muzzle offset, direction is bent from the muzzle to the eye ray's aim point. The projectile system's default for a free `{ yaw, pitch }` aim, so a bullet visibly comes from the barrel without missing the reticle. Needs a scene raycast to find the aim point (`convergeShot`); a bare `resolveShot` degrades to a straight muzzle ray. Passes an explicit `{ origin, direction }` aim through untouched. - `eye` ‚Äî `aim.origin` when present, else the shooter's entity position raised to eye height; the shot traces the shooter's sightline, so what the crosshair covers is what gets hit. - `legacy` ‚Äî `aim.origin` when present, else the shooter's raw entity position (feet). - `entity` ‚Äî always the shooter's entity position. - `entityOffset` / `muzzle` ‚Äî entity-local offset rotated by the shooter's yaw (muzzle on a weapon model). - `camera` ‚Äî explicit camera/reticle world origin (and optional direction override). - `world` ‚Äî absolute world origin.
- `aimDirection` (function): function aimDirection(aim: Aim): EntityPosition | null ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `aimSpreadDeg` (function): function aimSpreadDeg(aim: Aim): number ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `convergeShot` (function): function convergeShot(deps: ShotOriginDeps, from: string, aim: Aim, range: number, sightHit: (origin: EntityPosition, direction: EntityPosition) => EntityPosition | null, muzzleOffset?: EntityPosition): ResolvedShot | null ‚Äî Resolves a `converge` shot with scene knowledge: fires from the gun muzzle but bends the direction so the shot passes through the aim point the shooter's eye ray covers. `sightHit` casts the eye ray and returns where it lands (first impact), or `null` to fall back to a point `range` metres down the sightline. A `{ origin, direction }` aim is passed through unchanged (nothing to converge).
- `eyeHeightFromColliders` (function): function eyeHeightFromColliders(set: EntityColliderSet | null | undefined): number ‚Äî Eye height derived from a collider set: 90% of the tallest hitbox top, or the humanoid default when unknown.
- `resolveShot` (function): function resolveShot(deps: ShotOriginDeps, from: string, aim: Aim, policy: ShotOriginPolicy = { kind: "eye" }): ResolvedShot | null ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented

## @jgengine/core/combat/statusApplication

- `StatusApplicationInput` (interface): interface StatusApplicationInput ‚Äî Everything `resolveStatusApplication` needs; all data plus an injected RNG for reproducibility.
- `StatusApplicationOutcome` (interface): interface StatusApplicationOutcome ‚Äî The result of one status application: the outcome, the resulting instance (if any), and the roll.
- `StatusApplicationOutcomeKind` (type): type StatusApplicationOutcomeKind = | "applied" | "refreshed" | "stacked" | "replaced" | "immune" | "missed" | "ignored" ‚Äî Why an application did or did not take, plus the roll for provenance.
- `StatusApplicationScale` (interface): interface StatusApplicationScale ‚Äî Matchup-derived multipliers on the independent application axes. Each defaults to 1.
- `StatusApplicationSpec` (interface): interface StatusApplicationSpec ‚Äî A serializable, effect-agnostic description of a status a hit tries to apply: an opaque `status` id the game routes to its own effect/DoT pipeline, a base landing `chance`, and the duration/ticks/magnitude/attribution/stacking data the resolver needs. The engine never interprets `status` ‚Äî it is a reference, not a built-in condition.
- `StatusImmunity` (interface): interface StatusImmunity ‚Äî Statuses a target cannot receive at all ‚Äî the immunity half of application policy.
- `StatusInstance` (interface): interface StatusInstance ‚Äî A live status carried by a target ‚Äî the serializable, replicable state a status application mutates.
- `StatusStackPolicy` (type): type StatusStackPolicy = | { kind: "refresh" } | { kind: "stack"; max?: number; add?: number } | { kind: "replace" } | { kind: "ignore" } ‚Äî How a fresh application behaves when the target already carries the status. - `refresh` ‚Äî reset duration, hold the existing stack count. - `stack` ‚Äî add `add` stacks (default 1) up to `max`, and refresh duration. - `replace` ‚Äî overwrite duration and magnitude, stacks reset to 1. - `ignore` ‚Äî leave the existing instance untouched (first application wins).
- `resolveStatusApplication` (function): function resolveStatusApplication(input: StatusApplicationInput): StatusApplicationOutcome ‚Äî Resolve one status application: reject on immunity, roll landing chance against injected RNG, scale magnitude/duration by the matchup, then fold into any existing instance per the stacking policy. Deterministic given a seeded `rng`; pure over serializable data, so authority can run it and replicate only the returned `instance`.

## @jgengine/core/combat/thresholdCrossings

- `CrossingDirection` (type): type CrossingDirection = "falling" | "rising" ‚Äî The direction a crossing moved: `falling` = value decreased past the mark, `rising` = increased past it.
- `CrossingPolicy` (type): type CrossingPolicy = "once" | "repeat" ‚Äî Whether a threshold may fire repeatedly (`repeat`) or at most once per direction (`once`).
- `CrossingTrigger` (type): type CrossingTrigger = "falling" | "rising" | "both" ‚Äî Which directions should emit crossings.
- `ThresholdCrossing` (interface): interface ThresholdCrossing ‚Äî One emitted crossing: which threshold, which way, and the value span that produced it.
- `ThresholdCrossingConfig` (interface): interface ThresholdCrossingConfig ‚Äî Caller configuration for a threshold tracker.
- `ThresholdMark` (interface): interface ThresholdMark ‚Äî Generic numeric threshold-crossing tracker (#931).
- `ThresholdTracker` (interface): interface ThresholdTracker ‚Äî A stateful crossing tracker over one numeric source.
- `ThresholdTrackerSnapshot` (interface): interface ThresholdTrackerSnapshot ‚Äî Serializable tracker state for save/load.
- `createThresholdTracker` (function): function createThresholdTracker(config: ThresholdCrossingConfig): ThresholdTracker ‚Äî Track a numeric source against named thresholds and emit ordered crossing events with direction, hysteresis, once/repeat policy, and skipped-threshold handling. Deterministic and serializable; drives phase transitions, armor breaks, resource warnings, and any other threshold consumer.

## @jgengine/core/combat/vfxPresets

- `DEFAULT_VFX_PRESET` (const): const DEFAULT_VFX_PRESET: VfxPresetName ‚Äî The fallback flavor used when a `vfx()` call names an unknown preset ‚Äî so a typo still shows a spark instead of nothing.
- `VfxPreset` (interface): interface VfxPreset ‚Äî A named visual flavor for a one-shot `combat.vfx` burst: the archetype (`kind`), a `0xRRGGBB` tint, and optional default `durationMs`/`radius`. Purely presentation ‚Äî a preset never touches damage, range, or any authoritative value; it just answers "what does this attack look like" so a game does not hand-roll colors and archetypes.
- `VfxPresetName` (type): type VfxPresetName = keyof typeof vfxPresets ‚Äî A named key into {@link vfxPresets} (`"arrow" | "fireball" | "lightning" | "web" | "slash" | "shield" | "heal" | ...`).
- `resolveVfxPreset` (function): function resolveVfxPreset(name: string | undefined, overrides?: Partial<VfxPreset>): VfxPreset ‚Äî Resolve a named visual flavor into a concrete `{ kind, color, durationMs?, radius? }`, with any provided `overrides` winning field by field. An unknown name resolves to {@link DEFAULT_VFX_PRESET} rather than throwing, so a flavor typo degrades to a visible spark. Used by `ctx.scene.entity.vfx({ preset })`; call it directly when you need the raw numbers (e.g. to seed a retained `vfxInstance` from the same vocabulary).
- `vfxPresets` (const): const vfxPresets: { readonly arrow: { readonly kind: "projectile"; readonly color: 14207392; }; readonly bolt: { readonly kind: "projectile"; readonly color: 16777215; }; readonly fireball: { readonly kind: "projectile"; readonly color: 16738842; readonly durationMs: 460; }; readonly firebolt: { rea‚Ä¶ ‚Äî Ready-made visual flavors for the things players actually shoot, swing, and cast ‚Äî so `ctx.scene.entity.vfx({ preset: "arrow", from, to })` renders a visible bolt with zero tuning, and `"lightning"`, `"web"`, `"slash"`, `"shield"`, `"heal"`, `"explosion"` likewise just work. Each entry only picks a `kind` (`projectile | beam | nova | glow | spark`), a color, and a lifetime; it is visual vocabulary (like named CSS colors), not a gameplay archetype ‚Äî combat numbers stay in the caller's own effect/ability data. Any field passed to `vfx()` overrides the preset, and unknown names fall back to `spark` so a flavor typo still shows *something* rather than nothing.
## @jgengine/core/combat/weaponFire

- `FireCadence` (interface): interface FireCadence ‚Äî A minimal minimum-interval rate gate for a repeated action ‚Äî weapon fire, ability spam, any "no faster than N per second" rule. Deterministic and serializable: its whole state is the elapsed time since the last action, exposed via {@link FireCadence.elapsedMs}.
- `FireCadenceConfig` (interface): interface FireCadenceConfig ‚Äî Tuning for {@link createFireCadence}: the minimum interval between actions and whether it starts ready.
- `WeaponFireResult` (interface): interface WeaponFireResult<THit> ‚Äî The result of a {@link WeaponRuntime.fire} attempt. On `"fired"`, `hits` is whatever the caller's raycast returned and `resolutions` holds the portable damage provenance for each hit that produced a {@link DamageHitInput}; on any blocked status both are empty and no round was spent.
- `WeaponFireStatus` (type): type WeaponFireStatus = "fired" | "cooling" | "empty" | "reloading" ‚Äî Why a {@link WeaponRuntime.fire} attempt resolved the way it did.
- `WeaponRuntime` (interface): interface WeaponRuntime<TAim, THit> ‚Äî A composed weapon fire controller: a {@link FireCadence} gate, an optional {@link Magazine}, the caller's raycast, and portable {@link resolveDamageHit} damage resolution wired together. The caller retains ownership of models, animations, muzzle flash, audio, input, camera, aiming, and the raycaster itself.
- `WeaponRuntimeConfig` (interface): interface WeaponRuntimeConfig<TAim, THit> ‚Äî Configuration for {@link createWeaponRuntime}. Everything time/ammo-related is plain data; the two callbacks are where the caller keeps ownership of aiming, its own raycaster/projectile query, and how a hit maps to damage. There is deliberately no default weapon, projectile, or damage table here ‚Äî those are game content.
- `createFireCadence` (function): function createFireCadence(config: FireCadenceConfig): FireCadence ‚Äî Build a {@link FireCadence} rate gate. Reach for this instead of hand-tracking a `lastFiredAt` timestamp and comparing against a fire interval per game.
- `createWeaponRuntime` (function): function createWeaponRuntime<TAim, THit>(config: WeaponRuntimeConfig<TAim, THit>): WeaponRuntime<TAim, THit> ‚Äî Compose a portable weapon fire controller from the existing generic primitives ‚Äî a {@link FireCadence} gate, an optional {@link Magazine}, a caller-provided raycast, and portable {@link resolveDamageHit} damage resolution. No `GameContext`, entity store, renderer, or default weapon is involved; the caller owns aiming, its raycaster, and all presentation, and the result carries full damage provenance for authority to apply and replicate.
