## @jgengine/core/stats/spawnLevelStats

- `CreatureStatInstance` (interface): interface CreatureStatInstance<TStat extends string> ‚Äî A single creature's serializable stat record ‚Äî a reference to its species (`speciesId`), the current `level`, the per-stat count of distributed points, and how many post-capture domestic levels have been spent. Plain data: round-trips through JSON, save/load, and multiplayer sync. Resolve display values with {@link resolveSpawnStats}.
- `SpawnSpeciesDef` (type): type SpawnSpeciesDef<TStat extends string> = Record<TStat, SpawnStatDef> ‚Äî A species' stat table: every stat the creature owns keyed to its {@link SpawnStatDef}. The key order is the deterministic order random points are distributed over, so a serialized species definition reproduces the same rolls under the same rng sequence.
- `SpawnStatDef` (interface): interface SpawnStatDef ‚Äî Per-stat growth definition for one species. `base` is the level-1 value of the stat; `growth` is how much a single distributed point adds, expressed as a fraction of `base` ‚Äî so a point on a `growth: 0.1` stat is worth +10% of base. The resolved value is `base * (1 + points * growth)`.
- `applyCaptureEffectiveness` (function): function applyCaptureEffectiveness<TStat extends string>(species: SpawnSpeciesDef<TStat>, instance: CreatureStatInstance<TStat>, effectiveness: number, rng: () => number): CaptureResult<TStat> ‚Äî Apply a capture at the given effectiveness: compute bonus levels via {@link captureBonusLevels}, distribute those extra points randomly with the injected rng, and return the post-capture instance (level raised to the tamed total) alongside the granted bonus levels and tamed level. The input instance is not mutated.
- `captureBonusLevels` (function): function captureBonusLevels(level: number, effectiveness: number): number ‚Äî Bonus levels granted by a capture at the given effectiveness: `floor(level * clamp01(effectiveness) / 2)`. A perfect (1.0) capture grants half the wild level; a botched (0) capture grants none. Effectiveness outside `[0,1]` clamps.
- `resolveSpawnStats` (function): function resolveSpawnStats<TStat extends string>(species: SpawnSpeciesDef<TStat>, instance: CreatureStatInstance<TStat>): Record<TStat, number> ‚Äî Resolve an instance's live stat values from its distributed points: `value = base * (1 + points * growth)` per stat. Stats with zero points return their base.
- `restoreSpawnInstance` (function): function restoreSpawnInstance<TStat extends string>(data: CreatureStatInstance<TStat>): CreatureStatInstance<TStat> ‚Äî Rehydrate a creature stat instance from decoded data (e.g. `JSON.parse` output), returning a fresh deep copy the caller owns. Inverse of {@link snapshotSpawnInstance}.
- `rollSpawnInstance` (function): function rollSpawnInstance<TStat extends string>(speciesId: string, species: SpawnSpeciesDef<TStat>, level: number, rng: () => number): CreatureStatInstance<TStat> ‚Äî Roll a fresh wild creature at `level`: distribute `(level - 1)` points randomly across the species' stats using the injected rng and return a serializable {@link CreatureStatInstance}. Deterministic ‚Äî the same rng sequence yields the same point spread. Levels below 1 clamp to 1 (no points). Use `seededRng` from `../random/rng` for a persisted, reproducible stream.
- `snapshotSpawnInstance` (function): function snapshotSpawnInstance<TStat extends string>(instance: CreatureStatInstance<TStat>): CreatureStatInstance<TStat> ‚Äî Deep-copy an instance into a detached, serializable record ‚Äî the transport/save counterpart of {@link restoreSpawnInstance}. Copies the points map so later edits don't leak into the snapshot.
- `spendDomesticLevel` (function): function spendDomesticLevel<TStat extends string>(instance: CreatureStatInstance<TStat>, stat: TStat, options?: SpendLevelOptions): SpendLevelResult<TStat> ‚Äî Spend one earned post-capture level into `stat`, adding a single growth increment (one point) and raising both `level` and `domesticLevels`. When `maxDomesticLevels` is set and already reached, the spend is refused (`spent: false`) and the instance is returned untouched. Pure: returns a new instance rather than mutating the input.

## @jgengine/core/stats/statPool

- `StatPool` (interface): interface StatPool ‚Äî Serializable state for any bounded named resource. The caller defines what the resource means.
- `StatPoolAccess` (interface): interface StatPoolAccess<TOwnerId extends string = string, TStatId extends string = string> ‚Äî Structural bridge to caller-owned resource state. Implement it over an ECS, Redux/Zustand store, plain object, database-backed model, or JGengine's native entity stats without moving ownership into the SDK.
- `StatPoolAccessResult` (type): type StatPoolAccessResult = | ({ status: "ok" } & StatPoolChange) | { status: "rejected"; reason: string } ‚Äî Outcome of changing a pool through {@link StatPoolAccess}.
- `StatPoolChange` (interface): interface StatPoolChange ‚Äî Inspectable result of a bounded pool change.
- `StatPoolInput` (interface): interface StatPoolInput ‚Äî Input used to create a normalized pool. `current` defaults to `max`; `min` defaults to zero.
- `StatPoolPatch` (interface): interface StatPoolPatch ‚Äî Partial update applied without mutating the previous pool.
- `applyStatPoolDelta` (function): function applyStatPoolDelta<TOwnerId extends string, TStatId extends string>(access: StatPoolAccess<TOwnerId, TStatId>, ownerId: TOwnerId, statId: TStatId, amount: number): StatPoolAccessResult ‚Äî Read, transition, and write a caller-owned pool through the structural adapter. Positive amounts increase the resource; negative amounts decrease it. The adapter receives a complete replacement value, never a hidden mutation.
- `changeStatPool` (function): function changeStatPool(pool: StatPool, amount: number): StatPoolChange ‚Äî Pure bounded delta transition with the exact applied amount and boundary flags.
- `createStatPool` (function): function createStatPool(input: StatPoolInput): StatPool ‚Äî Normalize caller data into a complete plain-data pool.
- `patchStatPool` (function): function patchStatPool(pool: StatPool, patch: StatPoolPatch): StatPool ‚Äî Apply new bounds/current to a pool, clamping the result and leaving the input untouched.
